zh.Chelmsford, Essex, England: 英國艾可傑爾姆斯福德
zh.of Great Thorndon, Essex, England: 英國英格蘭素福爾可登
zh.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Tyrell是Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon和Jane de Borgate的兒子，出生在英國艾可。他於1328年與Jane Flambert在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1378年之後在英國艾可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Tyrell是Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon和Jane de Borgate的儿子，出生在英国艾可。他于1328年与Jane Flambert在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1378年之后在英国艾可。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tyrell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Tyrrell  .

variants.name: Tyrrell
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): perhaps a nickname from a derivative of Old French tirer ‘to pull’, in the same sense as French Tirand ‘one who pulls on the reins’, hence ‘obstinate, stubborn’. This name may have become confused with Turrell . A branch of this Anglo-Norman family has been in Ireland since the 12th century. Its name has been Gaelicized as Tirial.
English: habitational name from Tirril (Cumbria), which derives from Old Norse tyri ‘resinous wood’ + erg ‘shieling’.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Tyrell was born in Essex, England as the son of Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon and Jane de Borgate. He married Jane Flambert in 1328, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died after 1378, in Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Tyrell est né à Essex, Angleterre, fils de Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon et Jane de Borgate. Il a épousé Jane Flambert en 1328, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé après 1378, à Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Tyrell wurde in Essex, England, als Sohn von Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon und Jane de Borgate geboren. Er hat 1328 in England, United Kingdom Jane Flambert geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb nach 1378 in Essex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Tyrell è nato il nel Essex, England come figlio di Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon e Jane de Borgate. Lui sposato Jane Flambert nel 1328, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì dopo il 1378, nel Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de TyrellはイギリスエセックスでSir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of AvonとJane de Borgateの息子として生まれた。1328年にイギリスイギリスでJane Flambertと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1378年以後にイギリスエセックスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Tyrell(은)는 영국 에세스에서 Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon(와)과 Jane de Borgate의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1328년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Jane Flambert(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1378년 이후 영국 에세스에서 사망했다.

mn.of Great Thorndon, Essex, England: Thorndon, Sudfole, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Tyrell nasceu em Essex, Inglaterra filho de Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon e Jane de Borgate. Ele casou-se com Jane Flambert em 1328, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu após 1378, em Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Tyrell родился в местности эссекс, Англия. Его родителей звали Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon и Jane de Borgate. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Flambert в 1328 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер после 1378 г. в местности эссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Tyrell nació en Essex, Inglaterra como hijo de Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon y Jane de Borgate. Se casó con Jane Flambert en 1328, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió después de 1378, en Essex, Inglaterra.
